mainEntity.name: How long do I have to report a lifting injury at work in Utah before it's too late?

acceptedAnswer.text: In Utah, you must notify your employer of a lifting injury within 30 days to protect your right to benefits. While the formal filing deadline with the Labor Commission is generally eight years, failing to report the incident internally within the first month often leads to a claim denial.
acceptedAnswer.description:
In Utah, you generally have 30 days to notify your employer of a lifting injury. While the formal "statute of limitations" to file a claim with the Labor Commission is eight years from the date of injury (or the last payment of benefits), waiting to report the initial incident is the most common reason claims are denied. If you don't report it within 30 days, you risk losing your right to benefits entirely.

This timeline is strict because employers and insurance carriers need to investigate the scene and the task while details are fresh. 

Key Factors:
- The 30-day reporting window for notice to the employer.
- The 8-year statutory limit for formal filing.
- Exceptions for injuries that weren't immediately apparent.

You should report the injury in writing as soon as you feel pain, even if you think it might just be a minor strain. 

If you have already passed the 30-day mark without reporting, you need to speak with an attorney immediately to see if an exception applies to your case.
